payload.text:
Tạo 1 ứng dụng cho phép nhập vào 1 đường dẫn như này: https://platform-resources-staging.s3.ap-southeast-1.amazonaws.com/2048-px/696fa77152211892b20fbcf2.jpeg

thì sẽ tự động hiển thị danh sách total 6 ảnh:
https://platform-resources-staging.s3.ap-southeast-1.amazonaws.com/2048-px/696fa77152211892b20fbcf2.jpeg
https://platform-resources-staging.s3.ap-southeast-1.amazonaws.com/2048-nx/696fa77152211892b20fbcf2.jpeg
https://platform-resources-staging.s3.ap-southeast-1.amazonaws.com/2048-py/696fa77152211892b20fbcf2.jpeg
https://platform-resources-staging.s3.ap-southeast-1.amazonaws.com/2048-ny/696fa77152211892b20fbcf2.jpeg
https://platform-resources-staging.s3.ap-southeast-1.amazonaws.com/2048-pz/696fa77152211892b20fbcf2.jpeg
https://platform-resources-staging.s3.ap-southeast-1.amazonaws.com/2048-nz/696fa77152211892b20fbcf2.jpeg

mỗi ảnh sẽ hiện thumbnail, tên dạng "2048-px" và nút download => bấm vào sẽ tải ảnh xuống, và có nút download all để tải tất cả xuống cùng lúc.

**Defining the Scope**

I'm now focusing on defining the scope and functionality. The central concept is to generate related image URLs from a provided URL, following a specified pattern. I've broken this down into generating 6 variations, displaying them as thumbnails, and providing download options.


**Analyzing the Pattern**

I've zeroed in on the URL structure. The key is to grab the suffix and filename, and manipulate them. A regular expression or string manipulation will extract the suffix. For downloads, individually or as a ZIP, I'm thinking of triggering downloads or using `jszip`. The user interface design will be clean and modern. I'm imagining a Tailwind CSS-based layout: an input for the URL, a grid with thumbnails, download buttons for each, and a "Download All" button. Dark mode would be a great bonus.


**Defining Implementation Steps**

I'm now concretizing implementation details. I'll need `index.html`, `index.tsx`, `App.tsx`, and `components/ImageCard.tsx`. For utilities, `urlHelper.ts` will parse URLs, and I'll use `lucide-react` for icons and `framer-motion` for transitions. I'll stick with `jszip` for the "Download All" feature, after anticipating and resolving potential CORS complications with direct S3 downloads.


**Constructing the Core App**

I'm now turning to build the app's functionality. My plan is to start with permissive CORS access for the S3 bucket, a good starting point for my logic. I'll focus on the core image retrieval and download features. I'll refactor later, so I can construct a working minimum viable product (MVP).

entries.description: App metadata containing the name and description.
Main HTML entry point with Tailwind CSS and Font support.
Utility functions for parsing and generating asset URLs.
Component to display an individual asset thumbnail and download action.
Main application component with layout and business logic.
